summary.goal: Render markdown into beautiful, fully styled output — headings as headings, not raw ## markup. This is a VIEWER, not an editor.
summary.description:
The app is a VIEWER - it renders markdown into beautiful, fully styled output. The user explicitly stated: they want to see headings as headings, not raw ## markup. This is NOT a markdown editor.

## Core Requirement
- Fully rendered output (like a web page, not raw markdown)
- Beautiful typography with clear visual hierarchy
- Monospace fonts throughout (per theme system)

## Must Support
- Headings (h1-h6) with clear visual hierarchy (sized, weighted)
- Paragraphs, line breaks
- Bold, italic, strikethrough
- Ordered and unordered lists (nested)
- Links (clickable, open in default browser)
- Images (inline and referenced)
- Code blocks with syntax highlighting
- Inline code
- Tables (with alignment)
- Blockquotes
- Horizontal rules
- Task lists / checkboxes (rendered, not interactive)
- Math notation (LaTeX / KaTeX)
- Footnotes
- Definition lists
- HTML blocks (basic subset)

## Code Syntax Highlighting
- Decent highlighting for most-used languages
- Open source library that is minimal and fast
- Candidates: Tree-sitter (native Swift bindings), Splash (Swift-native), or Highlightr
- Cover top languages: Swift, Python, JS/TS, Go, Rust, Java, C/C++, Ruby, Shell, SQL, JSON, YAML, HTML/CSS

## Spec Compliance
- CommonMark spec as baseline
- GitHub Flavored Markdown (GFM) extensions
- Mermaid diagrams: stretch goal (requires JS runtime - architecture risk)

## Rendering Approach
- TextKit 2 + NSTextView (per Pipeline architecture)
- NOT WKWebView (too heavy, slow launch)
- Native attributed string rendering for speed
- Precomputed styles per theme

## Interactive Element UX (improvement)
- Links: underline on hover, pointer cursor, tooltip showing destination URL
- Images: click to open in Preview.app (optional)
- Task lists: rendered as visual checkboxes (not interactive)
- Wide tables: horizontal scroll within table container, not full-window scroll
- Footnotes: click footnote reference scrolls to footnote, back-link scrolls back

acceptance_criteria.description: Headings display as styled headings, not raw markdown syntax
Supports CommonMark and GitHub Flavored Markdown specs
Code blocks have syntax highlighting for major languages
Supports all listed elements: headings, lists, links, images, tables, blockquotes, etc.

observations: Must support full CommonMark + GFM spec
TextKit 2 attributed strings for native rendering
Mermaid diagrams are stretch goal — architecture risk with JS runtime
concerns: Table rendering in TextKit 2 is non-trivial — may need custom layout
Math notation may require WKWebView fallback

observations: FULLY RENDERED — not raw markdown source
Beautiful typography with clear visual hierarchy
Links: underline on hover, pointer cursor, tooltip
Wide tables: horizontal scroll within container

steps.title: Implement comprehensive AST node styling for all inline elements
steps.description: Extend the pipeline's MarkdownStyler (from pl-i004) to handle all inline markdown elements with full visual fidelity. Bold: apply .bold trait to monospace font. Italic: apply .italic trait. Strikethrough: NSAttributedString.Key.strikethroughStyle. Inline code: monospace font with distinct background color (slight tint from theme) and small padding effect via paragraph style. Links: apply .link attribute with URL, underline style, and link color from theme. Softbreaks and linebreaks: appropriate whitespace/newline handling per CommonMark spec.

acceptance_criteria.description: Bold text renders with bold font weight
Italic text renders with italic font trait
Strikethrough text has visible strikethrough line
Inline code has monospace font with tinted background
Links are clickable, underlined, and colored per theme
Nested formatting works (e.g., bold italic, bold code)

architecture.nesting: Font traits compose — bold + italic = boldItalic trait

steps.title: Implement list rendering with nesting and task lists
steps.description: Render ordered and unordered lists with proper indentation for nesting levels. Unordered lists: bullet character (e.g., Unicode bullet or dash) followed by content. Ordered lists: number followed by period. Nested lists indent progressively (each level adds ~20pt left margin via NSMutableParagraphStyle.headIndent and firstLineHeadIndent). Task lists (GFM extension): render checkbox character — filled square for checked, empty square for unchecked — these are display-only, not interactive. Support up to 6 levels of nesting.

acceptance_criteria.description: Unordered lists render with bullet markers at each level
Ordered lists render with sequential numbers
Nested lists indent progressively (up to 6 levels)
Task lists show checkbox characters (checked/unchecked, non-interactive)
List content aligns properly with hanging indent for multi-line items

design.paragraph_style: headIndent and firstLineHeadIndent for proper hanging indent

steps.title: Implement blockquote rendering with left border and tint
steps.description: Render blockquotes with a visible left border and subtle background tint, both colors sourced from the theme. Use a combination of NSMutableParagraphStyle for indentation and custom drawing for the left border. The border should be a solid line (~3pt wide) in the theme's blockquote border color, with the content area having a subtle background tint. Nested blockquotes stack the border and increase indentation. Content inside blockquotes retains all other styling (bold, links, etc.).

acceptance_criteria.description: Blockquotes display with visible left border (~3pt) in theme color
Blockquote content has subtle background tint from theme
Nested blockquotes stack borders and increase indentation
Inline formatting inside blockquotes works correctly

design.border_position: Left side, aligned with content indent
design.nesting: Additional border + indent per nesting level

architecture.implementation: Custom NSTextAttachment or paragraph style with custom drawing delegate for left border

steps.title: Implement table rendering with alignment and alternating rows
steps.description: Render GFM tables with proper column alignment, borders, and alternating row colors. This is one of the harder elements in TextKit 2 since NSTextView does not have native table support. Options: (1) Use tab stops in NSParagraphStyle for column alignment — works for simple tables; (2) Use NSTextTable and NSTextTableBlock — provides real table layout; (3) Render as a custom NSTextAttachment with a drawn table. Recommended: NSTextTable/NSTextTableBlock which is available in AppKit and integrates with TextKit. Apply theme colors for borders and alternating row backgrounds. Wide tables should be able to scroll horizontally within the text view.

acceptance_criteria.description: GFM tables render with visible borders in theme color
Column alignment (left, center, right) respected per GFM spec
Header row visually distinct (bold, different background)
Alternating row colors from theme applied
Tables with many columns do not break overall layout

architecture.approach: NSTextTable + NSTextTableBlock for native TextKit table support
architecture.fallback: Tab-stop alignment if NSTextTable proves problematic
architecture.header_style: Bold font, distinct background color from theme
architecture.wide_tables: Allow horizontal scroll within table container

steps.title: Implement horizontal rules and spacing elements
steps.description: Render horizontal rules (thematic breaks) as a visible divider line spanning the content width. Use either a custom NSTextAttachment that draws a line, or an attributed string with a strikethrough on whitespace. Apply theme's foreground color at reduced opacity. Add proper spacing: generous top and bottom margins around the rule. Also ensure consistent paragraph spacing throughout: headings get extra top margin for visual grouping, paragraphs get standard line spacing from theme.

acceptance_criteria.description: Horizontal rules render as visible divider lines
Proper spacing above and below horizontal rules
Heading top margins create clear visual grouping
Consistent paragraph spacing throughout document

steps.title: Implement link interaction behavior
steps.description: Make links interactive in the NSTextView. Links should: (1) show underline on hover via NSTextView's link tracking; (2) change cursor to pointing hand on hover; (3) open in the default browser when clicked (NSWorkspace.shared.open(url)); (4) show a tooltip with the destination URL on hover. For relative links in markdown, resolve them relative to the markdown file's directory. Cmd+click should follow system preference behavior.

acceptance_criteria.description: Clicking a link opens the URL in the default browser
Hovering over a link shows pointer cursor and underline
Tooltip shows full URL on hover
Relative links resolve to markdown file's directory

architecture.tooltip: NSTextView's link tooltip support or custom tracking area
architecture.resolution: Relative URLs resolved against markdown file URL

steps.title: Implement footnote rendering with bidirectional navigation
steps.description: Render footnote references as superscript numbers in the text and footnote definitions at the bottom of the document. Clicking a footnote reference scrolls to the definition. Each definition has a back-link that scrolls back to the reference. Use anchor-style internal links (NSAttributedString link attributes pointing to internal anchors). Footnotes are a cmark-gfm extension — ensure the extension is enabled in the parser.

acceptance_criteria.description: Footnote references render as superscript numbers
Footnote definitions render at bottom of document
Clicking reference scrolls to definition
Back-link in definition scrolls back to reference

architecture.superscript: NSAttributedString.Key.superscript or font size reduction + baseline offset
architecture.internal_links: Custom URL scheme (e.g., viewmd://footnote/1) for internal navigation

steps.title: Implement HTML block rendering (basic subset)
steps.description:
Handle HTML blocks and inline HTML that commonly appear in markdown. Support a basic subset:
,
, , ,
/ (rendered expanded), (keyboard shortcut styling), (highlight). Strip unsupported/dangerous HTML tags (script, style, iframe). Do not attempt full HTML rendering — this is a safety-first approach. Unsupported tags are stripped; their text content is preserved.

acceptance_criteria.description:
and
render as line breaks and horizontal rules
<sup> and <sub> render as superscript and subscript
<kbd> renders with keyboard-shortcut styling (border, background)
Dangerous tags (script, style, iframe) are stripped
Unsupported tags stripped but text content preserved

security.approach: Whitelist-based — only render known safe tags
